hypothesis: Increasing benign training volume (200k cap -> all unique benign) reduces the ~99% out-of-distribution false-positive rate on IoT-23 benign traffic.
result: REJECTED. Augmentation did not reduce OOD benign FPR; on the second capture (honeypot-5-1) it made the MLP substantially worse (0.369 -> 0.999).

data.augmented_parquet: data/cic_iot_2023_benignaug.parquet (5,342,156 rows; 4,248,253 attack unchanged + 1,093,903 unique benign)

in_distribution_cic_test.note: augmented numbers use the augmented test split (more benign); FPR/TPR are rates so comparable. Baselines are the locked depth-25 values.

preprocessor_fix.bug: models/preprocessor_random.joblib was stale; serving mis-scaled -> all-attack (in-dist FPR 1.0).
preprocessor_fix.fix: scripts/regenerate_preprocessor.py re-fits on the SEED train split; in-dist MLP FPR restored to 0.02.
preprocessor_fix.second_bug: load_dataset double-applied log1p (train) vs single (serving); fixed so Preprocessor is sole owner.

incomplete.8class_rf: augmented 8-class RandomForest retrain crashed on save (OSError errno 28, disk full); deferred to a later retrain. 8-class MLP trained (val macro-F1 0.6299). Not on the A/B critical path (cross-dataset eval is 2-class only).
